stem:
The table shows three values of x and their corresponding values of y, where y = f(x) + 6 and f is a quadratic function.

xy
11−5
1311
15−5

What is the y-coordinate of the y-intercept of the graph of y = f(x) in the xy-plane?
rationale: From the symmetric values at x = 11 and x = 15, the parabola has its vertex at x = 13 with y = 11. So f(x) + 6 = a(x − 13)² + 11. Using x = 11, y = −5: −5 = a(4) + 11, so a = −4. Therefore f(x) = −4(x − 13)² + 11 − 6 = −4(x − 13)² + 5. At x = 0: f(0) = −4(169) + 5 = −676 + 5 = −671. Hmm — re-derive: y-intercept of f is at x = 0: f(0) = −4(13)² + 5 = −676 + 5 = −671.
